physicalDetails.detail: Young human woman, blonde, blue-eyed, in practical travel robes with a dusty hem and boots grey with miles.
Hands marked by work: ink-stained fingers, calluses, and frost-split knuckles raw from her own magic.
Braid loose at the neck; tired at the edges but not soft, with a jaw set hard enough to show what courtesy is covering.
After six hours in the ward circle she looks held together by stubbornness: aching jaw, stiff legs, split knuckles reopened by fresh frost, and travel robes dirtied further by road and blood.
Mounts like a scholar forced to become fieldwork in public — competent enough to stay up, not graceful enough to hide the effort.
In Millhaven she is repeatedly on one knee in dirt or grain, hands dusted with barley and hair slipping loose at the nape while the crystal in her staff pulses without invitation.
Cold work shows physically when she strips the spoor: sweat beads along her hairline in the chill even while she holds herself steady enough for Sylvanas to read through her magic.
Chapter4 keeps the damage visible: frost-split knuckles, travel-creased blue robes, and a body held upright by focus while she braces staff and hand against stone through triage.

Chapter5 keeps the wear visible but functional: split knuckles silvered with frost at the window, a copied map lifting over the saddle horn, and her braid loosening farther at the nape as the night analysis deepens.
Chapter6 keeps her on the same grey Thalassian mare, using reins, saddle, and the mare's neck as physical anchors while headache and wards tighten around the Andorhal basin.

voiceMarkers.detail: Off-page reported speech suggests urgency, technical confidence, and impatience with anyone slowing the work.
Repeats the same core warning in different forms: the lines are wrong, rot is riding north, and time is being wasted.

Begins chapter2 talking smaller than she thinks, translating mage knowledge into soldier-sized pieces; once Sylvanas proves she has already followed the map, Jaina stops softening and speaks in sharper, fuller diagnostic language.
When frightened into instinct, explanation falls away and action takes over; when given a real listener, her cadence lengthens and becomes more exact rather than more diplomatic.

Chapter3 shows disciplined uncertainty rather than handwaving: this reads loudest, not earliest; something spread it through the whole village; if I hold it, can you read only the ground that answers.
With Miri she goes low and careful, asking permission before contact and pulling usable fragments out of panic without pretending the child can narrate cleanly.
Her speech turns into clean field medicine and clipped command: no false comfort, exact diet questions, immediate restrictions, and a colder professional cadence once grain proof locks into place.
She stops translating downward. In the saddle and over the table she speaks in quick revisions, probabilities, and practical next-book logic, trusting Sylvanas to track the whole chain.
By chapter6 she can brief a prince in hard operational terms—grain, body, death, organization—then cut straight to the ask when courtesy stops being useful.

beliefs.belief: Something dangerous is already moving north through existing magical channels, and the ward-stones are carrying evidence of it.
If she can gather enough evidence fast enough, acting alone is preferable to waiting for permission.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Still active at chapter open, but chapter2 proves it is no longer sufficient.

beliefs.belief: The contamination is no longer only magical infrastructure damage; it has entered soil, water, blood, and living tissue before death.
Being right is not enough; surviving this investigation requires Sylvanas's field judgment and armed control as much as Jaina's magical read.
The strongest magical trace is not automatically the first cause; discipline means refusing the easy answer when the evidence is only loud, not clean.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed by her granary read in chapter3.

beliefs.belief: Field truth has to be triangulated between magic, witness testimony, and spoor; none of those alone is enough any longer.
The plague is entering living bodies through ingestion, with repeated meals of tainted grain settling it deeper into the gut before it spreads outward.
Sylvanas's read of fear, routine, and supply is not supporting evidence but the other half of the method.
Severity tracks repeated dependence on shipped grain, not simple exposure alone.
beliefs.trueOrFalse: Confirmed at regional scale in chapter5.

beliefs.belief: Once a route is being scrubbed, fragments, duplicate books, and logistical habits matter as much as raw magical residue.
Sylvanas can follow the full analytical chain without having it softened or translated.
The plague is a deliberate campaign built from alchemy, necromancy, trade discipline, and repeated ingestion rather than a loose run of cursed grain.

The contamination is introduced during Andorhal-side packaging by trained necromantic-alchemical hands, and any case strong enough to matter now has to unite arcane mechanism with a live human witness.

formerBeliefs.belief: If she is correct enough and urgent enough, explanation alone should move sensible authority into action.
formerBeliefs.replacement: She has to give the full answer and let other kinds of competence carry the parts she cannot.

formerBeliefs.belief: If she can lay the case out cleanly enough for Arthas on his own soil, rank and personal history will force useful action.
formerBeliefs.replacement: Authority can hear the truth perfectly and still choose the visible war first; she needs proof strong enough to bypass belief.

goalsHistory.active: Get Sylvanas to hear the warning and let her return to the stones or at least take the contamination seriously.
goalsHistory.underlying: Stop the northward spread before institutions dismiss it into catastrophe.

goalsHistory.active: Reach Millhaven under escort and keep giving Sylvanas the clearest possible read before the contamination outruns them again.
goalsHistory.underlying: Stop the spread by pairing her diagnosis with people who can actually hold the road and the village.

goalsHistory.active: Keep riding south with the reduced party and continue separating the loudest traces from the truest ones before the plague outruns them again.
goalsHistory.underlying: Find the mechanism of spread in time to prevent more villages from becoming Millhaven.

goalsHistory.active: Ride southwest toward Andorhal with Sylvanas and get ahead of the grain network before the next delivery turns another village.
goalsHistory.underlying: Turn the plague from a village-scale emergency into a traceable supply-chain problem before more settlements become Millhaven.

goalsHistory.active: Stay on the southwest feeder road, keep the pattern legible while the enemy scrubs it, and reach the Barov-side books before they vanish.
goalsHistory.underlying: Turn the plague road from widening network into a trackable origin path that can carry them into Andorhal.

goalsHistory.active: Get inside Andorhal, seize surviving books, and prove the grain network at its source before the city can clean itself into silence.
goalsHistory.underlying: Force action with evidence that does not depend on anyone choosing to trust her first.

goalsAtChapterEnd.active: Read the treatment building and its carrier trace during the same-night operation, then test any prisoner's story against the magical evidence.
goalsAtChapterEnd.underlying: Turn the plague's mechanism into proof that reaches beyond one local cell and points toward whoever prepared the system.

emotionalStateHistory.state: Furious, exhausted, and tightly controlled, with fear buried under urgency and professional conviction.
Shaken, grim, and still deeply irritated by custody, but newly respectful of Sylvanas as a mind she cannot afford to discount.
Grim, depleted, and fully engaged. Millhaven has hardened her caution, but it has also made her stop waiting to be invited before she contributes what matters.
Exhausted, angry, and more collaborative than she likes admitting. Davan's death hardens her, but the method is finally shared instead of hoarded.
Tired, sharpened, and less defensive in shared work. She is still dry and urgent, but the collaboration now feels habitual instead of negotiated.
Corrected, angry, physically strained, and resolute. Arthas listened and refused; now only the city and its records matter.

emotionalStateAtChapterEnd.state: Tired, exact, and quietly reliant. She is working at full speed inside a split command structure now, confident enough to take her half alone and fold it back into Sylvanas's capture plan.

decisionsThisChapter.detail: Crosses the ward line alone before dawn to inspect the southern stones herself.
Keeps working even when challenged and initially refuses to give her name.
Admits she came without Kirin Tor approval because nobody believed her and accepts that the honest version is strategically worse but necessary.
Accepts armed escort and custody terms because magical expertise without road survival is useless.
Stops translating downward after Sylvanas proves she has already read the map and the field correctly.
After the deer attack, gives Sylvanas the full diagnosis and keeps riding toward Millhaven instead of trying to solve the road alone.
Reads the granary, the well, and the village saturation without overclaiming grain as the source, even when it is the easiest answer available.
Examines Miri carefully without touching until invited and confirms the child is uncontaminated.
Helps pull usable fragments from Miri's testimony instead of demanding a clean chronology from a traumatized child.
Uses magic to strip background contamination from the road cut so Sylvanas can read the spoor cleanly.
At chapter end she rides into the next investigation without waiting for Sylvanas to ask whether her read matters.
Uses stabilization wards to buy hours for infected villagers while she maps the pattern through living bodies.
Cross-references patient condition against diet and household routine until bread consumption locks the answer into place.
Orders the contaminated grain and all fresh bread pulled from use the moment the proof is clean enough to act on.
Leaves a written ward sequence for a later garrison mage instead of staying to manage the village herself, then commits to Andorhal at first light.
Revises her first route instinct as soon as Sylvanas proves the upstream logic, folding her notes to match without vanity.
Combines contamination notes with manifests and village infirmary reports until the regional pattern becomes legible.
Confirms that the same quiet necromantic touch used on Davan's operation has been used again on the Millbrook Spur overseer.
Pushes the investigation toward duplicate books and large-buyer copies once she recognizes the active cleanup pattern.
Agrees to the southwest feeder road when the Barov-side clustering locks into place.
Locks the field model into one brutal sequence—grain, body, death—and admits that only the final answering force remains dark.
Brings the full case to Arthas at Strahnbrad and asks for riders or any authority who can seize the Andorhal records before the grain keeps moving.
Stops arguing with official refusal once Arthas has made his choice and recommits the hunt to records, towers, and signatories inside Andorhal itself.
Uses Corin's access, seal, and records to isolate the contamination to city-side packaging and accepts a split operation where Sylvanas works the human target while Jaina takes the magical one.
Insists on doing both halves of the case at once — building read and live capture — then writes the sequence, fallback, and signal plan herself.

abilitiesDemonstrated.ability: Performs field analysis on ward-stones using chalk work, notes, crystals, and frost magic.
Pushes cold magic hard enough to leave visible frost burns on her own hands.
Reads when ward systems are damping a magical signal and immediately feels the stronger contamination once the anchor stone is pulled.
Traces necromantic contamination from leylines into dirt, shallow water, blood, and living tissue, distinguishing pre-death activity from simple corpse animation.
Casts rapid frost control under panic, pinning a charging buck in place before Sylvanas kills it.
Reads saturation across an entire village and distinguishes the strongest concentration from the likely first source.
Detects that a stone cellar kept one survivor below the taint even after the rest of Millhaven was soaked through.
Filters magical noise out of damaged spoor so physical tracking can isolate the undead movement pattern.
Lays narrow stabilization wards into living patients, slowing progression without pretending the delay is a cure.
Combines magical diagnosis with diet and labor correlation until she can prove ingestion through bread rather than guess at it.
Reads the broken liquid agent in the granary as refined necromantic-alchemical work rather than rustic hedgecraft.
Diagnoses the speed and hopelessness of Davan's poison fast enough to know interrogation is lost before denial wastes time.
Writes a warding method cleanly enough that another trained mage can reproduce it under field conditions.
Integrates magical diagnosis with manifests, route marks, and sickness tallies at regional scale instead of village scale only.
Recognizes a repeat necromantic silencing method from days-old residue on a body meant to pass for ordinary death.
Recalculates route risk quickly enough to keep the pursuit moving even as the evidence changes shape under them.
Builds a stable field model of the plague as grain preparation, bodily conditioning, and death-triggered completion while openly marking the final mechanism as unknown.
Maintains riding wards under escalating ambient corruption strong enough to press through them as the party nears Andorhal.
Packages weeks of field evidence into a concise command briefing that a military leader can read in terms of roads, wagons, and time-to-impact.
Reads live contamination inside a working granary city without alerting the whole yard, isolating a necromantic carrier laid on during repacking rather than at harvest.
Proves the carrier survives milling, flouring, and baking, refining the plague model from broad theory into mechanism.
Builds a tight operational plan on the fly, calculating fallback routes, watch timing, and how arcane verification can interrogate a prisoner's testimony.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Sylvanas and Farstriders
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: The mage, the prisoner, or Jaina Proudmoore once identity is confirmed.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Sylvanas Windrunner
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Mostly you and role-based framing rather than intimacy; even when convinced, Sylvanas keeps her at professional distance.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Miri
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Treats Jaina as a quiet adult helper rather than a commander, which lets Jaina work in a gentler register than she uses with the rangers.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Tomas Brek and the village child
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Both reduce her first to the role — mage — before trusting the person behind it; chapter4 keeps her public identity functional rather than social.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Sylvanas Windrunner
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: By chapter5 Sylvanas thinks of her as Jaina naturally during working scenes, which marks settled professional respect rather than intimacy.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Arthas Menethil
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Uses Jaina immediately, with warmth first and command pressure second; the familiarity is real, but it does not change his decision.

howOthersAddressHer.speaker: Corin
howOthersAddressHer.pattern: Proudmoore — respectful Kirin Tor formality carrying relief that someone senior has finally arrived to read what he has been documenting.

howSheAddressesOthers.pattern: Begins from formal mental distance — Ranger-General, a soldier, an obstacle — then shifts internally to Sylvanas once respect replaces condescension.
Uses the child's name immediately and speaks in permission-shaped questions rather than diagnostic commands.
Uses Brek's surname and direct medical imperatives once he proves competent enough to act on them immediately.
By chapter4 she speaks her half of the answer to Sylvanas as to another specialist, without explanatory softening or lecture cadence.
She answers Sylvanas directly and dryly, without explanatory softening or ceremonial distance; the rhythm is specialist to specialist.
Begins on his first name and shared history, then turns flat and strategic once the conversation becomes a refusal about roads, riders, and grain.
Uses his name immediately and shifts into brisk colleague-to-colleague instruction, treating him like a useful junior mage rather than a clerk to be soothed.

bodyLanguagePatterns.detail: Stands too fast for indifference, meets scrutiny directly, and looks like someone whose attention is still half at the ward-stones instead of the room.
Keeps her hands rigidly still when honesty costs her; once released, she immediately leans into the staff and the road rather than into relief.
Has a bad habit of kneeling into danger to touch the problem directly while other people cover the edges for her.
In Millhaven she repeatedly grounds herself with one hand against dirt, floor, or wall before letting the magic speak through the other.
She lowers her profile around Miri, setting the staff aside and keeping her body language deliberately nonthreatening.
Grounds herself physically against walls, desk edges, and the staff between bursts of magic; realization often arrives through a tightened hand on stone or wood.
She works with both palms flat on maps and papers, fingers shifting between note stacks as if holding the pattern in place physically while she thinks.
Chapter6 turns reins, table edge, and the mare's neck into anger-braces; she parks fury in her body and keeps it useful instead of letting it spill into the room.

In Andorhal she keeps flattening pages, folded samples, and maps under her hands as if holding the answer in place physically; when she is sure, she goes still in a way that ends the argument before anyone speaks.

knowledge.detail: Knows the southern ward-stones are carrying damage or contamination from farther south rather than simply showing signs of an outside blast.
Has field notes, rune rubbings, and a map marking ley crossings and northward spread near population routes.
Knows the elven ward network had been muting the contamination; outside the pass the signal is broader, deeper, and physically painful.
Learns the corruption has crossed from deep magical channels into soil, shallow water, blood, organs, and living behavior before clean death.
Recognizes Millhaven as the first major convergence where three channels bunch hard enough for the contamination to become visibly catastrophic.
Finally understands that Sylvanas reads maps, routes, threat geometry, and tactical survival faster than Jaina had allowed for.
Learns Millhaven is soaked through at every level she can test, with the granary strongest but not decisively first.
Confirms Miri is physically clean because the cellar stayed below the taint.
Hears and files the survivor's fragment about sweetness in bread and a newer grain seller without upgrading it to proof.
Sees that her own magical read and Sylvanas's spoor work can combine into a single usable answer about the dead moving north.
Learns the living-stage outbreak clusters first in the gut and worsens fastest in the villagers who eat the most bread.
Confirms grain is the carrier moving the plague into people, and that repeated meals deepen the damage.
Learns a new supplier pattern now links this village to Millhaven's weaker grain clue.
Sees the plague in concentrated liquid form before it is worked through grain, proving deliberate preparation.
Learns a live watcher was stationed in the granary and carried poison for capture, proving structured oversight behind the village spread.
Leaves chapter4 with Andorhal fixed as the next supply-chain target.
At least eleven settlements can now be named with certainty inside the same contaminated trade pattern, with dozens more likely tied to it.
The worst outbreaks are the places that keep buying shipped grain rather than falling back on local harvest.
The same precise necromantic method that silenced Davan's side of the operation has also been used on the Millbrook Spur overseer.
If Andorhal's main books are gone, copies may survive with large buyers, quartermasters, or other institutions that need their own records.
The Barov-linked stops cluster on the southwest side of the basin strongly enough to make that feeder road the best live route.
The plague pattern now reads as grain preparing the body over time, death closing the lock, and some still-unnamed answering force completing the transformation.
Whoever built the plague knew alchemy, necromancy, and trade logistics well enough to hide mass murder inside ordinary bread.
Arthas hears the warning, understands its scale, and still refuses to divert riders from the visible war in front of him.
The farewell Safe roads. What's left of them. has now recurred on two grain-road encounters weeks apart, making it a pattern rather than a one-off oddity.
Andorhal is visibly alive and working even while the corruption presses across her wards in a broad sheet from miles away.
Corin has been tracking tainted Andorhal shipments for weeks; his tied-lattice tests and copied manifests prove three contaminated receipt points in the granary district.
The grain arrives clean and is tainted during packaging or transfer inside Andorhal, with the carrier bound close to the inner sacks rather than only to the canvas.
The treatment work shows disciplined dark scholarship rather than hedge magic, and the final wake-up still depends on the host body finishing part of the process alive.

arc.lie: If she is right enough and works fast enough, she can force reality to matter before politics catches up.
arc.truth: Seeing the pattern is not enough; she needs other people to carry the parts of the problem she cannot.
arc.currentPosition: Chapter7 turns that correction into practice. Jaina is no longer trying to keep the whole answer inside her own sightline. She takes her lane cleanly, trusts Sylvanas to take the other, and builds one case out of both halves.
